page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes an Intel processor with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor architecture includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and a large 260 MB L3 cache. It features advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) for accelerated vector and matrix operations, alongside cryptographic accelerations like AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the n4-standard-4 delivers moderate single-core capabilities but exhibits limitations under multi-threaded workloads. Single-core performance is average, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 1561.99 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1807.0. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4100.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 3213.05. Memory performance is also constrained, with uncached read and write bandwidths measuring 32.36 GB/sec and 21.01 GB/sec respectively, placing it in the lower tier of memory performance. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform moderately well, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 28.23 GB/sec and Passmark CPU encryption scoring 3440.67.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the n4-standard-4 is positioned as a cost-efficient option for entry-level general-purpose workloads that do not demand high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-threaded processing. It is well-suited for static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 6.53 GB/sec, as well as basic text processing and light single-threaded applications. However, it is less optimal for database operations, software compilation, or complex multi-threaded tasks like ray tracing, where benchmarks show lower relative performance. It can handle lightweight machine learning tasks, such as text generation on small 135M models at 118.57 tokens/sec, but struggles with prompt processing on larger models.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core, it features a dedicated CPU allocation and a 260 MB L3 cache. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1807.0, but weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. This instance is best suited for lightweight workloads such as static web serving, basic text processing, and single-threaded applications, offering a cost-efficient entry point for general-purpose computing where high-throughput multi-threading and local storage are not required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-4 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support, offering balanced single-core performance for entry-level workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-4 is a general-purpose x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel processors.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel processors and advanced matrix extensions for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Dedicated Intel x86_64 processor with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions
Average single-core performance suitable for static web serving and text processing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight general-purpose and single-threaded applications
